cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out between what times Leslie could have gone to the movies. The problem gives me several time intervals when Leslie was seen doing different things, and the movies closed after 7pm. Let me start by listing out all the given information. First, Leslie woke up at 6am. Then Susan saw Leslie on a rooftop from 6am to 7am. So from 6 to 7 am, Leslie was on the rooftop. Then Mary saw Leslie working at the office from 12pm to 2pm. So between noon and 2pm, Leslie was at the office. Then Steven saw Leslie getting coffee at the cafe from 2pm to 7pm. Wait, that's a long time—five hours. So from 2pm until 7pm, Leslie was at the cafe getting coffee. And the movies closed after 7pm, so Leslie couldn't have gone after that. Now, the question is when could Leslie have gone to the movies. The movies are presumably open before 7pm, but Leslie has to be free during the time of the movie. So I need to check which time intervals are not covered by the activities Leslie was doing. Let me break down the timeline: - 6am to 7am: on the rooftop with Susan - 7am to 12pm: what's happening here? The next activity is at 12pm (office), so from 7am to 12pm, there's no mention of Leslie's activities. So that's a possible window. - 12pm to 2pm: at the office with Mary - 2pm to 7pm: at the cafe with Steven - After 7pm: movies closed, so can't go then. So the only time slots not accounted for are 7am to 12pm. Because from 6-7am, Leslie was on the rooftop. Then until 12pm, no one saw Leslie. Then from 12-2pm at the office, then 2-7pm at the cafe. Therefore, the only possible time Leslie could have gone to the movies is between 7am and 12pm. Let me check the options. Option A is 7am to 12pm. Option B is 2pm to 7pm, but during that time Leslie was at the cafe.
